section_header: b5. expand access to public and private low-cost financing

parent.policy_statement: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
parent.verbatim_text:
## B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing

## Components of the Strategy

- Integrate energy requirements and resources into affordable housing deals...
- Integrate energy performance into underwriting...
- Expand access to financing...
- Expand energy savings performance contracting for public sector buildings...
- Create a revolving loan fund...
parent.section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple specific bullet actions beneath it, suitable as a parent policy.

subs.policy_statement: Require that by no later than 2023, all new construction projects receiving HCR Tax Credit funding be high-performance and all-electric buildings per HCR Sustainability Guidelines for New Construction, and streamline access to incentives through housing agencies for regulated affordable housing decarbonization.
subs.verbatim_text: - Integrate energy requirements and resources into affordable housing deals: ... For example, by no later than 2023, all new construction projects that receive Tax Credit funding through HCR will be required to be high-performance and all-electric buildings, as required in HCR's Sustainability Guidelines for New Construction. The State should also continue to streamline access to all incentives and resources for regulated affordable housing building decarbonization to go through the housing agencies...
subs.parent_policy_name: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified deadline (no later than 2023) and explicit requirement; clearly a bullet under B5.

subs.policy_statement: Provide support for lenders to underwrite to energy performance standards; consider updating HCR underwriting standards to account for reduced operational costs from high-performance building standards informed by data from existing projects.
subs.verbatim_text: - Integrate energy performance into underwriting: The State should provide support for lenders to underwrite to energy performance standards and applicable regulatory requirements. HCR should consider updating its underwriting standards... to take into account potential reduced operational costs stemming from the new high performance buildings standards.
subs.parent_policy_name: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (underwriting support/updates) under B5.

subs.policy_statement: Provide greater access to low-cost financing products for upgrades (including for low-income homeowners and buildings in Disadvantaged Communities), including financing for non-energy improvements needed for code compliance and electrification; explore low-interest financing coupled with credit enhancement/insurance; prioritize CDFIs and credit unions.
subs.verbatim_text: - Expand access to financing: The State should provide greater access to low-cost financing products for upgrades, including for low-income homeowners and buildings located in Disadvantaged Communities. This financing also should be available for non-energy improvements needed for code compliance and to enable electrification... The State should also explore new mechanisms... coupled with credit enhancement or insurance. The State should prioritize support for financing products made available by community development financial institutions and credit unions...
subs.parent_policy_name: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete expansion of financing access with target groups and mechanisms; listed under B5.

subs.policy_statement: Enact enabling legislation to expand energy savings performance contracting for State and municipal buildings, schools, and other public facilities, including expanding eligible measures and payback terms and allowing retention of some cost savings.
subs.verbatim_text: - Expand energy savings performance contracting for public sector buildings: The State should enact enabling legislation to expand the use of energy savings performance contracting to support implementation of emissions reduction upgrades in State and municipal buildings, P-12 schools, and other public facilities... Changes to existing statute should expand the eligible list of measures... expand the allowable payback term... allow... deferred maintenance needs, and allow a State agency or authority to request to keep a portion of cost savings...
subs.parent_policy_name: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
subs.extraction_rationale: Binding mechanism via proposed legislation; detailed statutory changes.

subs.policy_statement: Create a revolving loan fund for building decarbonization and reuse of buildings and building materials, implemented through a bond-issuing government authority.
subs.verbatim_text: - Create a revolving loan fund: The State should create a revolving loan fund for building decarbonization and the reuse of buildings and building materials... This would be implemented through a bond-issuing government authority.
subs.parent_policy_name: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B5. Expand Access to Public and Private Low-Cost Financing
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific financial mechanism and implementation approach; bullet under B5.

section_header: b6. support development of thermal energy networks

parent.policy_statement: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks (strategy framework).
parent.verbatim_text:
## B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks

## Components of the Strategy

- Workforce training for gas sector workers...
- Develop appropriate regulations and permit fees...
- Streamline access to public and utility rights of way...
- Support public-private partnerships...
- Prioritize public sector support...
- Explore thermal energy networks as part of the gas system transition...
parent.section_header: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple listed component actions; appropriate parent.

subs.policy_statement: Provide workforce training and transition support for gas sector workers to install/operate thermal energy networks, coordinating PSC/DPS, DOL, Office of Just Transition, utilities and unions.
subs.verbatim_text: - Workforce training for gas sector workers to operate thermal energy networks: ... The PSC and DPS, DOL, the Office of Just Transition, and other State agencies should work together with utilities and unions... to help connect trained gas workers with new thermal energy network projects, on training and skills development...
subs.parent_policy_name: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific training/transition action under B6.

subs.policy_statement: Develop geothermal well regulations and revise statutory financial security and permit fee provisions to reduce burden for geothermal wells >500 feet, including allowing DEC to set requirements/fees in regulation and developing geothermal/stratigraphic well regulations.
subs.verbatim_text:
- Develop appropriate regulations and permit fees for geothermal wells greater than 500 feet deep: ... The following actions should be taken... 
- DEC should develop regulations for geothermal and stratigraphic wells...
- Remove the dollar amounts from ECL §§ 23-0305... and allow DEC to establish financial security requirements in regulation...
- Remove the fixed dollar amount in ECL § 23-1903... and allow DEC to develop an appropriate permit fee schedule in regulation.
subs.parent_policy_name: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks
subs.extraction_rationale: Regulatory/statutory change actions are explicitly enumerated.

subs.policy_statement: Develop clear processes/rules for thermal energy networks to access public and utility rights-of-way and establish default compensation levels for affected property owners; provide transparent access to heat sources/sinks.
subs.verbatim_text: - Streamline access to public and utility rights of way as well as heat sources/sinks: The State, in partnership with local governments, should develop clear processes and rules for thermal energy network projects to access already established public and utility rights of way. In addition, the State should establish appropriate default compensation levels intended to compensate property owners in these rights of way... State and local governments also should provide simple and transparent access to heat sources and sinks...
subs.parent_policy_name: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific administrative rules/compensation commitments under B6.

subs.policy_statement: Emphasize public sector support for thermal energy networks serving LMI/affordable/public housing and Disadvantaged Communities, including requiring at least one pilot per utility territory be located in a Disadvantaged Community per the Utility Thermal Energy Networks and Jobs Act.
subs.verbatim_text: - Prioritize public sector support for thermal energy networks that serve LMI housing and buildings in Disadvantaged Communities: ... The Utility Thermal Energy Networks and Jobs Act... requir[es] that at least one thermal energy network pilot project per utility service territory must be located in a Disadvantaged Community.
subs.parent_policy_name: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B6. Support Development of Thermal Energy Networks
subs.extraction_rationale: Contains an explicit requirement (at least one pilot per territory in a DAC) tied to statute.

section_header: b8. invest in workforce development

parent.policy_statement: B8. Invest in Workforce Development (buildings sector) (strategy framework).
parent.verbatim_text:
## B8. Invest in Workforce Development

## Components of the Strategy

- Expand training...
- Curricula and career services...
- Prioritize Disadvantaged Communities and other priority populations...
parent.section_header: B8. Invest in Workforce Development
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple component bullets; extract as parent.

subs.policy_statement: Increase training investments for incumbent and new clean energy workers via training infrastructure, career pathways, apprenticeships, on-the-job training, and industry partnerships in specified priority areas.
subs.verbatim_text: - Expand training: Training for incumbent and new clean energy workers and adjacent industries needs to be increased dramatically, through investments in training infrastructure/delivery, career pathways, direct-entry Pre-apprenticeship and Registered Apprenticeship programs, on-the-job-training, and industry partnerships.
subs.parent_policy_name: B8. Invest in Workforce Development (buildings sector) (strategy framework).
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific workforce intervention and delivery mechanisms listed; sub-bullet under B8.

subs.policy_statement: Encourage building decarbonization curricula and career services in State-funded and private education, including P-12, technical schools, apprenticeships, and university programs.
subs.verbatim_text: - Curricula and career services: The State should encourage building decarbonization curricula and career services in State-funded and private education including P-12, technical schools, apprenticeships, and engineering and architecture programs at universities.
subs.parent_policy_name: B8. Invest in Workforce Development (buildings sector) (strategy framework).
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit education-system action under B8.

subs.policy_statement: Prioritize Disadvantaged Communities and low-income residents for training and job placement via community-to-employment pipelines, wrap-around services, and labor standards mechanisms (pre-apprenticeships, apprenticeships, CBAs, OJT funding); increase MWBE/SDVOB/cooperative participation via funding and certification assistance.
subs.verbatim_text: - Prioritize Disadvantaged Communities and other priority populations: The State should prioritize Disadvantaged Communities and low-income residents for training and job placement by creating community-to-employment pipelines... pared with wrap-around services... The State should increase ranks of MWBEs... SDVOBs, and worker cooperatives through increased funding for workforce training, business development support, and certification assistance...
subs.parent_policy_name: B8. Invest in Workforce Development (buildings sector) (strategy framework).
subs.extraction_rationale: Targeting plus specific mechanisms and funding commitments; sub-bullet under B8.

section_header: b9. scale up public awareness and consumer education

parent.policy_statement: B9. Scale Up Public Awareness and Consumer Education (strategy framework).
parent.verbatim_text:
## B9. Scale Up Public Awareness and Consumer Education

## Components of the Strategy

- Scale up campaigns...
- Create strategic partnerships...
- Prioritize Disadvantaged Communities...
- Publicize leaders...
- Provide technical resources...
parent.section_header: B9. Scale Up Public Awareness and Consumer Education
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple action bullets; suitable parent.

subs.policy_statement: Support and scale up multilingual, culturally appropriate public and consumer education campaigns on building decarbonization options, incentives, financing, and upcoming code/standards requirements.
subs.verbatim_text: - Scale up campaigns: The State should support and scale up multilingual, culturally appropriate public and consumer education efforts through large-scale, coordinated awareness... campaigns... Topics... would emphasize available incentives... available low-cost financing products, and information on upcoming applicable codes and standards requirements.
subs.parent_policy_name: B9. Scale Up Public Awareness and Consumer Education (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B9. Scale Up Public Awareness and Consumer Education
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (campaigns) with defined content; sub-bullet under B9.

subs.policy_statement: Create strategic partnerships (including with utilities) to promote decarbonization and sunset messaging that promotes fossil natural gas as a 'cleaner' choice.
subs.verbatim_text: - Create strategic partnerships: The State should create strategic partnerships... The State should also partner with utilities to promote decarbonization and sunset messaging that promotes fossil natural gas as a 'cleaner' choice.
subs.parent_policy_name: B9. Scale Up Public Awareness and Consumer Education (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B9. Scale Up Public Awareness and Consumer Education
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit partnership action; sub-bullet under B9.

subs.policy_statement: Fund and expand community hubs and prioritize education/technical assistance for Disadvantaged Communities, including maintaining the New York Energy Advisor website as a one-stop shop for LMI households.
subs.verbatim_text: - Prioritize Disadvantaged Communities: ... NYSERDA's and the State's electric and gas utilities' commitment to maintain the New York Energy Advisor website as a 'one-stop shop'... The State should fund and expand community hubs to offer education, resources, local contractors, technical assistance, and program navigator support.
subs.parent_policy_name: B9. Scale Up Public Awareness and Consumer Education (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B9. Scale Up Public Awareness and Consumer Education
subs.extraction_rationale: Resource allocation ('fund and expand') and specific tool (website) targeting DAC/LMI; sub-bullet.

subs.policy_statement: Provide technical assistance toolkits and resources (including audits, playbooks, capital planning support) and develop case studies for three transition pathways: full electrification, phased electrification, and electrification readiness.
subs.verbatim_text: - Provide technical resources: The State should provide technical assistance and resource toolkits... free in-home or virtual audits... The State should develop case studies showing the feasibility, performance, and costs for three paths...: full electrification, phased electrification, and electrification readiness.
subs.parent_policy_name: B9. Scale Up Public Awareness and Consumer Education (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B9. Scale Up Public Awareness and Consumer Education
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific deliverables (toolkits, audits, case studies) under B9.

section_header: b11. reduce embodied carbon from building construction

parent.policy_statement: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction (strategy framework).
parent.verbatim_text:
## B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction

## Components of the Strategy

- Lead by example in State projects...
- Make embodied carbon transparent...
- Follow lower-carbon specifications...
- Set reduction targets for projects...
- Incorporate embodied carbon budgets into permitting...
- Encourage building reuse...
- Support RD&D...
- Expand in-state manufacturing for products...
- Incorporate embodied carbon specifications into incentive programs...
- Leverage federal efforts and policy development...
parent.section_header: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with extensive bullet list; parent appropriate.

subs.policy_statement: Require Environmental Product Declarations for structural materials (where available) and require embodied carbon modeling/tools and standardized methodologies/database adoption via GreenNY Council.
subs.verbatim_text: - Make embodied carbon transparent: In design specifications, the State should require Environmental Product Declarations for structural building materials where available and require the use of available modeling software and design tools for calculation of the project's embodied carbon budget. The State, through the GreenNY Council, should adopt methodologies for calculation of carbon reduction...
subs.parent_policy_name: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete procurement/design requirements; sub-bullet under B11.

subs.policy_statement: Set embodied carbon reduction targets for projects below the established mean embodied carbon budget after establishing baselines.
subs.verbatim_text: - Set reduction targets for projects: Subsequently, the State should set a target embodied carbon reduction level for projects that is below the established mean embodied carbon budget, as illustrated over the previous years.
subs.parent_policy_name: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit target-setting action under B11.

subs.policy_statement: Require embodied carbon budgets be submitted with permits for commercial/institutional new construction: immediately for State entities and no later than 2025 for local government entities; provide funding for training/resources for designers and permitting entities.
subs.verbatim_text: - Incorporate embodied carbon budgets into permitting: The State should require an embodied carbon budget to be submitted as part of the permit process for all commercial and institutional new construction... immediately for State entities and no later than 2025 for local government entities. The State should provide funding for training and resources...
subs.parent_policy_name: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B11. Reduce Embodied Carbon from Building Construction
subs.extraction_rationale: Contains a deadline (no later than 2025) and specific permitting requirement plus funding.

section_header: b12. advance a managed and just transition from reliance on hydrofluorocarbon use

parent.policy_statement: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use (strategy framework).
parent.verbatim_text:
## B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use

## Components of the Strategy

- Provide education and training...
- Update regulations, codes, and standards...
- Support the HFC phase-out in food stores...
- Phase out high-GWP HFCs...
- Research health effects and environmental impacts...
- Support RD&D...
parent.section_header: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple distinct policy components; parent appropriate. (Not duplicating earlier general HFC transition policy; these are additional specific actions.)

subs.policy_statement: Promulgate regulations for proper disposal of HFCs in existing equipment and support those regulations with training on handling, maintenance, and disposal protocols.
subs.verbatim_text: - Provide education and training: ... DEC should promulgate regulations regarding proper disposal of HFCs already in use in existing equipment and such regulations should be supported by training installers and contractors on handling, equipment maintenance, and disposal protocols.
subs.parent_policy_name: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use
subs.extraction_rationale: Regulatory action plus supporting training; concrete and enforceable.

subs.policy_statement: Update relevant codes (including mechanical code) to allow low-GWP alternatives and promulgate DEC regulations requiring refrigerant reclamation/destruction at end-of-life with verification/reporting and leak detection for certain commercial refrigeration; provide economic support for transition (e.g., incentives for equipment or compensation for reclamation).
subs.verbatim_text: - Update regulations, codes, and standards: As soon as possible, the State should update relevant codes, including the mechanical code, to allow the use of low-GWP alternatives... DEC should promulgate regulations requiring reclamation or destruction of refrigerants from appliances at end-of-life, with verification and reporting, and require leak detection for certain commercial refrigeration... the State should provide economic support (such as... incentives... or compensation...)
subs.parent_policy_name: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use
subs.extraction_rationale: Multiple concrete code/regulatory requirements plus support mechanisms.

subs.policy_statement: Develop incentives (utility rebates and grant programs) to support adoption of natural refrigerants in food stores, prioritizing substantial funding for equipment installation in Disadvantaged Communities and for independent/small chains.
subs.verbatim_text: - Support the HFC phase-out in food stores: ... NYSERDA, DPS, and DEC should coordinate to develop incentives such as utility rebates and grant programs to support the adoption of natural refrigerants in food stores. Incentives are particularly needed to fund a substantial portion of the installation of new equipment in existing stores in Disadvantaged Communities or stores operated by independent companies or small chains...
subs.parent_policy_name: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific incentive instruments and targeting; actionable coordination directive.

subs.policy_statement: Expand 6 NYCRR Part 494 by establishing a GWP threshold that decreases over time and addressing leakage in existing equipment; align state policy with anticipated EPA measures and other U.S. Climate Alliance states.
subs.verbatim_text: - Phase out high-GWP HFCs: DEC should expand the scope of 6 NYCRR Part 494... including through the establishment of a GWP threshold that decreases over time... and addressing leakage in existing equipment... DEC should align New York policy with anticipated federal (EPA) policy measures... as well as with other U.S. Climate Alliance states...
subs.parent_policy_name: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: B12. Advance a Managed and Just Transition from Reliance on Hydrofluorocarbon Use
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit regulatory expansion direction with defined mechanism (declining GWP threshold).

section_header: e1. retirement and/or repurposing of fossil fuel fired facilities

parent.policy_statement: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities (strategy framework).
parent.verbatim_text:
## E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities

## Components of the Strategy

- Assessment and determination of emissions reduction targets...
- Promulgation of emissions regulations...
- Regular and transparent resource planning...
parent.section_header: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple component bullets; parent appropriate.

subs.policy_statement: Determine potential GHG and co-pollutant reductions from fossil fuel generation by 2030 and set interim emissions reduction targets/timeline, with stakeholder input and biennial evaluation every two years starting in 2024.
subs.verbatim_text: - Assessment and determination of emissions reduction targets: The PSC, DEC, NYSERDA, and the New York State Energy Planning Board should work in coordination to determine the potential for GHG emission and co-pollutant reductions from fossil fuel generation by 2030 and set a corresponding timeline for interim emissions reduction targets... The effectiveness... shall be evaluated every two years starting in 2024 and adjusted accordingly...
subs.parent_policy_name: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities
subs.extraction_rationale: Time-bound target-setting process and periodic evaluation requirement; explicit agencies.

subs.policy_statement: Assess and promulgate emissions regulations/options to reduce emissions from fossil fuel-fired generating units to the maximum extent practicable while maintaining reliability, with three-year effectiveness evaluations.
subs.verbatim_text:
- Promulgation of emissions regulations: DEC should assess regulatory options to reduce emissions from fossil fuel-fired generating units to the maximum extent practicable... Once completed, DEC should follow SAPA in promulgating the identified regulation(s).

- Consistent with SAPA, the effectiveness of the regulations should be evaluated every three years.
subs.parent_policy_name: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities
subs.extraction_rationale: Clear regulatory commitment plus review cycle.

subs.policy_statement: Conduct biannual PSC reviews starting in 2024 of the renewable energy program and resource mix to support emissions targets and evaluate retirement/repurposing of fossil generation; complete a 2023 NYC fossil facility retirement/redevelopment blueprint including specified community and workforce measures.
subs.verbatim_text: - Regular and transparent resource planning: The PSC will conduct biannual reviews of the renewable energy program and electric system resource mix starting in 2024... As part of this review process, the PSC should evaluate options to retire and/or repurpose existing fossil fuel electric generation facilities. In 2022, the State initiated the development of a blueprint... by 2030... will be completed in 2023... The blueprint will also include detailed analysis and planning to address the impacts on communities and workers.
subs.parent_policy_name: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities
subs.extraction_rationale: Contains explicit deadlines (starting 2024; completed in 2023) and concrete planning actions.

subs.policy_statement: Consider and implement market mechanisms to remove fossil generation, including a Clean Dispatch Credit program requiring load-serving entities to purchase increasing amounts annually, with protections/prioritization for Disadvantaged Communities.
subs.verbatim_text: - Investigate and implement options to develop market mechanisms to assist in the removal of fossil fuel-fired generating facilities from the system... Specifically, the State should consider a clean dispatch program that creates Clean Dispatch Credits... Load-serving entities would be required to purchase increasing amounts of Clean Dispatch Credits annually... New York must ensure Disadvantaged Communities are not disproportionately burdened and prioritize reductions...
subs.parent_policy_name: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: E1. Retirement and/or Repurposing of Fossil Fuel Fired Facilities
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific market mechanism with requirement on LSEs; explicit equity constraint.

section_header: e2. accelerate growth of large-scale renewable energy generation

parent.policy_statement: E2. Accelerate Growth of Large-Scale Renewable Energy Generation (strategy framework).
parent.verbatim_text:
## E2. Accelerate Growth of Large-Scale Renewable Energy Generation

## Components of the Strategy

- Evaluate and adjust...
- Support successful programs...
- Identify facilitating transmission and distribution needs...
- Establish permitting goals...
- Explore...
- Labor and workforce development...
parent.section_header: E2. Accelerate Growth of Large-Scale Renewable Energy Generation
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple component bullets.

subs.policy_statement: Support Build Ready and the Accelerated Renewable Energy Growth and Community Benefit Act by funding and hiring adequate staff in ORES and relevant agencies to ensure efficient procurement/permitting.
subs.verbatim_text: - Support successful programs: The State should continue to support successful programs and regulatory changes, such as Build Ready and the Accelerated Renewable Energy Growth and Community Benefit Act through funding and hiring adequate staff in ORES and other relevant State agencies...
subs.parent_policy_name: E2. Accelerate Growth of Large-Scale Renewable Energy Generation (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: E2. Accelerate Growth of Large-Scale Renewable Energy Generation
subs.extraction_rationale: Resource allocation/staffing commitment; sub-bullet under E2.

subs.policy_statement: ORES should establish a non-binding annual MW permitting goal that complements Tier 1 procurement levels.
subs.verbatim_text: - Establish permitting goals: ORES should establish a non-binding metric or goal with respect to MWs of renewable energy that should be permitted each year in such an amount that complements the Tier 1 request for proposals procurements.
subs.parent_policy_name: E2. Accelerate Growth of Large-Scale Renewable Energy Generation (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: E2. Accelerate Growth of Large-Scale Renewable Energy Generation
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific permitting goal-setting action with metric (MW per year).

section_header: e6. deploy existing storage technologies

parent.policy_statement: E6. Deploy Existing Storage Technologies (strategy framework).
parent.verbatim_text:
## E6. Deploy Existing Storage Technologies

## Components of the Strategy

- Provide increased funding for energy storage deployment...
- Incorporate energy storage into energy delivery and transmission planning...
- Work with the NYISO...
parent.section_header: E6. Deploy Existing Storage Technologies
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple component bullets.

subs.policy_statement: Create a dedicated funding mechanism and binding targets for energy storage similar to CES as soon as practicable and no later than end of 2023, prioritizing funding to projects benefiting frontline communities.
subs.verbatim_text: - Provide increased funding for energy storage deployment: The PSC should consider methods... to initiate a new docket that sets new binding targets and creates a dedicated funding mechanism that is similar to the CES for storage as soon as practicable and no later than the end of 2023. Funding should be prioritized to projects that benefit frontline communities...
subs.parent_policy_name: E6. Deploy Existing Storage Technologies (strategy framework).
subs.extraction_rationale: Deadline (end of 2023) and binding targets/funding mechanism; clear action.

section_header: e7. invest in transmission and distribution infrastructure upgrades

parent.policy_statement: E7. Invest in Transmission and Distribution Infrastructure Upgrades (strategy framework).
parent.verbatim_text:
## E7. Invest in Transmission and Distribution Infrastructure Upgrades

## Components of Strategy

- Transmission and distribution expansion...
- Strategic investment...
- SF6 emissions...
- Hosting capacity...
- Renewable Energy Zones...
- Upgrades for offshore wind...
- Multi-port infrastructure...
- Education...
parent.section_header: E7. Invest in Transmission and Distribution Infrastructure Upgrades
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple actionable bullets; parent appropriate.

subs.policy_statement: Adopt DEC regulations to reduce SF6 emissions and establish a timeline to phase out new SF6 equipment, coordinating with other U.S. Climate Alliance states.
subs.verbatim_text: - SF6 emissions: DEC should adopt regulations to reduce SF6 emissions and establish a timeline for phasing out new SF6 equipment. New York should also collaborate with other U.S. Climate Alliance states to align policies...
subs.parent_policy_name: E7. Invest in Transmission and Distribution Infrastructure Upgrades (strategy framework).
subs.section_header: E7. Invest in Transmission and Distribution Infrastructure Upgrades

section_header: af10. advance agricultural nutrient management

parent.policy_statement: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
parent.verbatim_text: Farms across New York will continue to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution to waterways while achieving desired yield and quality through continued and expanded nutrient management planning and implementation on crop fields, hay fields, pastures, orchards, vineyards, and other agricultural lands receiving nutrients.
parent.section_header: AF10. Advance Agricultural Nutrient Management
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy (AF10) with a defined objective and a bulleted 'Components of the Strategy' list; qualifies as an umbrella program/strategy.

subs.policy_statement: Increase funding and technical assistance for nutrient management planning and implementation through the AEM Framework and associated programs (including CRF and AgNPS), including funding to quantify climate and co-benefits for farm-level GHG accounting and reductions.
subs.verbatim_text: - Expand funding and technical assistance: AGM and SWCC should increase support for planning, technical assistance, and soil health or nutrient management practice implementation through the AEM Framework and associated programs, including the CRF and Agricultural Nonpoint Source Abatement and Control (AgNPS) water quality program. This should include funding to quantify climate and co-benefits related to farm-level GHG emission accounting and reduction strategies.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific implementation action under AF10 with responsible agencies and resource/funding expansion.

subs.policy_statement: Engage and expand nutrient management program participation by seeking feedback from historically unrepresented and beginning farmers and removing participation obstacles (e.g., focus groups, surveys, farm-to-farm education).
subs.verbatim_text: - Engage with and expand program participants: AGM, SWCC, and SWCDs should seek feedback from historically unrepresented and beginning farmers who are not currently engaged in practices and programs to remove obstacles for participation (such as holding focus groups, surveys, and farm-to-farm education).
subs.parent_policy_name: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete engagement actions and target groups stated as 'should' under AF10.

subs.policy_statement: Expand cost-share eligibility for equipment needed to implement advanced soil health and nutrient management practices, emphasizing access for historically unrepresented and beginning farmers.
subs.verbatim_text: - Expand cost-share eligibility for historically unrepresented farmers and capital-intensive equipment: AGM and SWCC should expand cost-share eligibility for equipment needed by farms to implement more advanced soil health and nutrient management practices. Emphasis should be on improved access to technical and financial support for historically unrepresented and beginning farmers.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit expansion of cost-share eligibility and equity emphasis under AF10.

subs.policy_statement: Continue and enhance workforce training for technical assistance providers, planners, farmers, and farm workers to support nutrient management implementation.
subs.verbatim_text: - Enhance workforce training: AGM and SWCC should continue and enhance training for technical assistance providers, agricultural planners, farmers, and farm workers.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific training expansion commitment listed as a component under AF10.

subs.policy_statement: Support expanding capacity of custom farming service providers to aid on-farm implementation of nutrient management practices.
subs.verbatim_text: - Support implementation services: AGM and SWCC should support expanding capacity of custom farming service providers to aid on-farm implementation of nutrient management practices.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
subs.extraction_rationale: Clear support action for service provider capacity under AF10.

subs.policy_statement: Increase the use of improved monitoring methods for nutrient management (crop yield measurement, nitrogen use efficiency, and Whole Farm Nutrient Mass Balances) and document/share benefits with stakeholders.
subs.verbatim_text: - Increase use of existing tools: AGM should work with partners such as Cornell CALS, CCE, and others to increase the use of improved methods of monitoring performance via crop yield measurement, nitrogen use efficiency, and Whole Farm Nutrient Mass Balances (for farm-wide nitrogen management). These benefits of nutrient management should be documented and shared with farmers, policymakers, and the public.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specifies tools/metrics to increase and an action to document/share outcomes.

subs.policy_statement: Collaborate with industry-led nutrient management initiatives (e.g., 4R Nutrient Stewardship) and initiate/expand nitrogen efficiency and yield crop contests.
subs.verbatim_text: - Collaborate with industry led initiatives: AGM and partners should collaborate with industry led nutrient management initiatives and services, such as the 4R Nutrient Stewardship Program . They should also initiate and expand nitrogen efficiency and yield crop contests for peer-to-peer competition and informational opportunities.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific collaboration mechanism and contest expansion stated under AF10.

subs.policy_statement: Implement long-term funding support for nutrient management applied research and outreach.
subs.verbatim_text: - Increase funding for applied research: AGM should implement long-term funding support for nutrient management applied research and outreach (such as management approaches and technology).
subs.parent_policy_name: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
subs.extraction_rationale: Resource allocation (long-term funding support) explicitly stated under AF10.

subs.policy_statement: Increase outreach to farmers on New York research/technical standards and increase consumer outreach on nutrient management benefits such as water quality improvements.
subs.verbatim_text: - Increase outreach: The State should increase outreach to all farmers with the research and technical standards used in New York. The State should also increase outreach to consumers on the benefits of nutrient management, such as water quality improvements.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF10 advances agricultural nutrient management by continuing and expanding nutrient management planning and implementation to reduce N2O emissions and nitrogen pollution while maintaining yields across New York agricultural lands.
subs.extraction_rationale: Two explicit outreach commitments under AF10.

section_header: af11. adopt soil health practice systems

parent.policy_statement: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
parent.verbatim_text: This strategy focuses on reducing net GHG emissions as well as increasing carbon sequestration and other environmental benefits through adoption of soil health management practice systems (such as combinations of cover and double crops, conservation crop rotations, perennial crops, prescribed grazing, and nutrient management), also referred to as regenerative agricultural practices.
parent.section_header: AF11. Adopt Soil Health Practice Systems
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with a defined focus and an enumerated 'Components of Strategy' list.

subs.policy_statement: Increase financial support for current soil health programs (including expanded CRF funding, AEM Base, AgNPS, payment rates, access/equity measures), develop a soil health standard, and establish an annual goal for common practices (including urban soils awareness/support).
subs.verbatim_text: - Expand funding for current programs: AGM and SWCC should increase financial support for currently available and implemented practices. This includes expanded funding for CRF, AEM Base, AgNPS, increased payment rates, increased access, building equity into programs, reducing barriers that limit access in applications, increased technical assistance, encouraging adoption of a system of practices, developing a soil health standard, and establishing an annual goal for common practices. This should also include increasing awareness and support for urban soils and agriculture.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
subs.extraction_rationale: Detailed package of funding, standards, and goal-setting actions under AF11.

subs.policy_statement: Seek feedback from under-engaged groups/communities (including urban soils/operations) via engagement activities to improve access to soil health practices and programs.
subs.verbatim_text: - Engage with and expand program participants: AGM and SWCC should seek feedback from groups/communities not currently engaged in practices and programs (such as holding focus groups, surveys, farm-to-farm educational events and addressing urban soils and urban agricultural operations). Improving access reflects the need to ensure that all farmers can take part in these practices and programs.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific engagement activities and objective under AF11.

subs.policy_statement: Increase adoption of soil health practices on rented/leased land by engaging, educating, and incentivizing landowners.
subs.verbatim_text: - Increase adoption of soil health practices on rented lands: AGM should increase practice adoption on rented and leased land. AGM should seek feedback regarding support needed for farmers not currently engaged in practices and programs and engage, educate, and incentivize landowners to increase adoption of practices on land they rent to farmers.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete actions aimed at rented land adoption under AF11.

subs.policy_statement: Support converting annual cropland to perennial hay/pasture and forests where appropriate and continue research/trials on perennial grains efficacy in New York State.
subs.verbatim_text: - Increase perennial plant systems: AGM and SWCC should support converting annual cropland to perennial hay land/pasture and forests where appropriate (such as steep slopes and highly erodible lands). This is a current effort supported through the State's AgNPS and CRF programs. Research and trials should continue to determine the efficacy of perennial grains systems in New York State.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific land conversion support plus ongoing research commitment under AF11.

subs.policy_statement: Conduct comprehensive on-farm planning to include carbon sequestration goals along with GHG emissions, nutrient management, water quality, and soil health.
subs.verbatim_text: - Increase integrated planning efforts: AGM and SWCDs should conduct comprehensive on-farm planning to include carbon sequestration goals, GHG emission, nutrient management, water quality, and soil health.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific planning intervention under AF11.

subs.policy_statement: Support continued development and implementation of precision/digital agricultural tools and sustainable intensification to increase yields on current cropland and reduce stress on marginal cropland.
subs.verbatim_text: - Increase use of precision and digital agricultural tools: AGM should support continued development and implementation of precision/digital agricultural tools and sustainable intensification, which is the sustainable increase in yields on current cropland to reduce stress on marginal cropland to support this mitigation strategy.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
subs.extraction_rationale: Clear technology support action under AF11.

subs.policy_statement: Develop tools to verify benefits and invest in remote sensing to quantify adoption of practices and environmental benefits.
subs.verbatim_text: - Develop tools for quantification of benefits: AGM, SWCC, and partners such as Cornell CALS should develop tools for verification of benefits and invest in remote sensing to quantify adoption of practices and environmental benefits.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
subs.extraction_rationale: Verification/remote sensing investment commitment under AF11.

subs.policy_statement: Establish and maintain a comprehensive RD&D strategy for monitoring and verification of soil health addressing additionality and permanence to support State climate goals and enable federal/private funding.
subs.verbatim_text: - Support research for monitoring and verification: AGM, SWCC, and partners such as Cornell CALS should establish and maintain a comprehensive RD&D strategy for monitoring and verification of soil health that address additionality and permanence to support State climate goals and enable federal and private funding of GHG emissions mitigation practices.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit RD&D strategy commitment under AF11.

subs.policy_statement: Establish and maintain a comprehensive research strategy in soil health to develop innovative practices (e.g., enhanced rock weathering, biochar) that increase sequestration and scale adoption.
subs.verbatim_text: - Support research for new innovative practices: AGM and partners such as Cornell CALS should establish and maintain a comprehensive research strategy in soil health to bring new practices and approaches (such as enhanced rock weathering and biochar) that increase sequestration rates, productivity, other environmental benefits, and scale for adoption.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
subs.extraction_rationale: R&D commitment including examples under AF11.

subs.policy_statement: Identify soil health practice systems that can generate revenue/added value and identify diverse public and private funding sources.
subs.verbatim_text: - Develop a business case for practices: The State should identify practice systems that can generate revenue and/or added value to the farm, as well as identify a variety of public and private funding sources.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
subs.extraction_rationale: Economic development/funding identification action under AF11.

subs.policy_statement: Conduct consumer outreach campaigns and marketing efforts to make regenerative practice efforts visible (including expanding regenerative practices in programs like New York Grown & Certified).
subs.verbatim_text: - Educate consumers: AGM should make efforts visible to the public through outreach campaigns making information more available, expand regenerative agricultural practices in marketing programs (such as New York Grown & Certified), and improve information provided to the public to help customers understand the practices involved in products they purchase.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific consumer education/marketing actions under AF11.

subs.policy_statement: Expand education/outreach to farmers and farmworkers to support adoption and encourage coupling practices into systems, including peer-to-peer networking.
subs.verbatim_text: - Educate farmers and farmworkers: AGM, SWCC, and SWCDs should expand education and outreach to include all farmers and farmworkers and to support practice adoption and encourage coupling of practices into systems for maximum benefit. They should support farm-to-farm and peer-to-peer networking to elevate long-term adoption of soil health management practice systems (such as through local farmer discussion groups).
subs.parent_policy_name: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
subs.extraction_rationale: Detailed education/networking interventions under AF11.

subs.policy_statement: Support agricultural and soil health instruction in schools and connect students with farms and ecological benefits of healthy soils.
subs.verbatim_text: - Educate students: AGM, SWCC, SWCDs, and partners such as Cornell CALS and CCE should support agricultural and soil health instruction in schools and connect students with farms and farmers and the knowledge of the ecological benefit of healthy soils.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF11 promotes adoption of soil health practice systems (regenerative practices) to reduce net GHG emissions and increase carbon sequestration through combinations of soil health management practices supported via AEM/CRF and related programs.
subs.extraction_rationale: Education program support under AF11 with responsible parties.

parent.verbatim_text: Adding trees into areas of agricultural production increases carbon sequestration and other environmental benefits. Current programs, technical services, and support should be leveraged to increase agroforestry adoption while new programs, increased investment in technical support, and capacity will be necessary.

subs.parent_policy_name: AF12 increases adoption of agroforestry by leveraging existing programs and expanding investment and technical support to overcome barriers and increase carbon sequestration.

subs.parent_policy_name: AF12 increases adoption of agroforestry by leveraging existing programs and expanding investment and technical support to overcome barriers and increase carbon sequestration.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific continuation/emphasis across named programs under AF12.

section_header: af14. monitor and benchmark agricultural greenhouse gas emissions

parent.policy_statement: AF14 establishes annual monitoring and benchmarking of agricultural GHG mitigation, sequestration, and adaptation performance, including farm-level benchmarking and aggregated public reporting.
parent.verbatim_text: Annual monitoring and benchmarking of GHG emissions mitigation, carbon sequestration, and adaptation performance across applicable areas of management on farms in New York is critical to determining success in meeting targets and providing further insight into what strategies lead to the greatest achievements.
parent.section_header: AF14. Monitor and Benchmark Agricultural Greenhouse Gas Emissions
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with detailed components including funding, methods, outreach, and data delivery.

subs.policy_statement: Establish funding for an agricultural benchmarking and monitoring program for GHG emissions via a partnership including Cornell CALS, CCE, SWCDs, farmer groups, and industry groups.
subs.verbatim_text: - Establish funding: AGM and DEC should establish funding for an agricultural benchmarking and monitoring program for GHG emissions. This should be a partnership effort among groups such as Cornell CALS, CCE, SWCDs, famer groups, and agricultural industry groups.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF14 establishes annual monitoring and benchmarking of agricultural GHG mitigation, sequestration, and adaptation performance, including farm-level benchmarking and aggregated public reporting.
subs.extraction_rationale: Direct funding commitment under AF14.

subs.policy_statement: Develop methods for monitoring and benchmarking agricultural emissions, including staffing and an advisory committee, with benchmarking at farm, county, and State levels using anonymous comparisons.
subs.verbatim_text: - Monitoring and benchmarking: AGM should develop methods for monitoring and benchmarking (including program staff and advisory committee). Benchmarking at the farm level for farmer use should be based on comparisons with the farm's historical performance as well as the performance of similar types of farms (each kept anonymous). Benchmarking should also occur at the county and State level with aggregated, anonymous data based on comparisons with our historical performance.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF14 establishes annual monitoring and benchmarking of agricultural GHG mitigation, sequestration, and adaptation performance, including farm-level benchmarking and aggregated public reporting.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific methodological design commitments under AF14.

subs.policy_statement: Introduce the monitoring and benchmarking program to farmers and farm advisors via outreach.
subs.verbatim_text: - Outreach: AGM and SWCC should introduce monitoring and benchmarking program with farmers and farm advisors.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF14 establishes annual monitoring and benchmarking of agricultural GHG mitigation, sequestration, and adaptation performance, including farm-level benchmarking and aggregated public reporting.
subs.extraction_rationale: Outreach action explicitly listed under AF14.

subs.policy_statement: Deliver confidential farm-scale data summaries and aggregated public summaries on BMP implementation and estimated GHG mitigation/sequestration, and develop additional performance metrics via an advisory committee/expert panel.
subs.verbatim_text: - Make data available: AGM should deliver data summaries of BMP implementation and associated estimates of GHG mitigation or sequestration levels for confidential farm-scale use and aggregated summaries for public use. Additional key performance metrics would be developed by the advisory committee/expert panel.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF14 establishes annual monitoring and benchmarking of agricultural GHG mitigation, sequestration, and adaptation performance, including farm-level benchmarking and aggregated public reporting.
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete data delivery and metric development commitments under AF14.

section_header: af18. expand markets for sustainably harvested wood products

parent.policy_statement: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.
parent.verbatim_text: Use of durable, long-lasting wood products, especially as a substitute for fossil fuel-intensive products like steel, concrete, brick, or vinyl, can reduce overall emissions for construction projects.
parent.section_header: AF18. Expand Markets for Sustainably Harvested Wood Products
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple component actions; serves as umbrella policy.

subs.policy_statement: Promote long-term durable wood products that store carbon and substitute for fossil-fuel-based materials, and incentivize longer-lived products while avoiding disincentives for residue/low-grade markets.
subs.verbatim_text: - Promote carbon storing materials: SUNY ESF, WPDC, ESD, and DEC should promote longterm, durable wood products that store carbon and are substitutes for energy efficiency materials that are fossil fuel-based (such as cellulosic fiber insulation and hemp insulation replacing foams and research potential on uses for residues from wood and hemp product creation). In general, more long-term durable products should be incentivized over production of shorter-term and shorter-lived products; however, the market for wood residues and low-grade wood should not be disincentivized to prevent the degradation of existing forests and to allow for improved forest management practices that enhance sequestration and climate resiliency.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete promotion and incentive guidance under AF18.

subs.parent_policy_name: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.

subs.verbatim_text: - Develop standards: NYSERDA should work with SUNY ESF, Cornell CALS, and DEC to set standards and specifications for a minimum portion of harvested wood products, such as mass timber or wood flooring, in new construction in certain State-funded/supported buildings and infrastructure projects when New York's supply chain can cost-effectively meet the demand.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific standards-setting with minimum portion requirement under AF18.

subs.policy_statement: Revise State procurement specifications to expand eligibility for qualifying wood products and set minimum standards for wood use in new State-funded construction and infrastructure projects as feasible.

subs.parent_policy_name: AF18 expands markets for sustainably harvested durable wood products and substitutes in construction by promoting materials, codes, standards, procurement, incentives, supply chains, and R&D.
subs.extraction_rationale: Procurement rule revisions and minimum standards under AF18.

subs.extraction_rationale: Specific barrier removal and incentive creation with example uses under AF18.

section_header: af19. develop a sustainable biomass feedstock action plan for bioenergy and low-carbon products

parent.policy_statement: AF19 develops a Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan for bioenergy and low-carbon products, including sustainability guidelines, feedstock definitions, incentives, and pathway identification to support emissions limits and avoid localized pollution.
parent.verbatim_text: A Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan will identify feedstock volumes and production methods that utilize New York biomass resources (wood and wood processing wastes, agricultural crops and waste materials, organics in municipal solid waste, animal manure, and wastewater treatment byproducts) in a sustainable, emissions-reducing and substitution-maximizing manner.
parent.section_header: AF19. Develop a Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan for Bioenergy and Low-Carbon Products
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with components specifying guidelines, definitions, incentives, and system development actions.

subs.policy_statement: Establish rigorous sustainability guidelines and metrics for bioenergy products using life cycle analyses, including air quality/health impact analysis and best practices to minimize impacts (especially in Disadvantaged Communities).
subs.verbatim_text: - Develop sustainability guidelines: NYSERDA, AGM, and DEC should establish rigorous energy, GHG, and environmental sustainability guidelines and metrics for bioenergy products using life cycle analyses. Sustainability guidelines should also include the analysis of potential air quality and health impacts of producing and utilizing bioenergy products and include best practices to minimize these impacts, especially for Disadvantaged Communities.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF19 develops a Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan for bioenergy and low-carbon products, including sustainability guidelines, feedstock definitions, incentives, and pathway identification to support emissions limits and avoid localized pollution.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific guideline-setting requirement under AF19.

subs.policy_statement: Define sustainable feedstock production for bio-based processing to determine volumes and practices that minimize emissions, including cost-effectiveness and assessment of using former agricultural/underused lands.
subs.verbatim_text: - Define sustainable feedstocks: NYSERDA should define sustainable feedstock production for bio-based processing to determine feedstock volume and practices that minimize total emissions as part of a biomass action plan. This should include an evaluation of cost-effectiveness and an assessment of activating former agricultural and underused lands (including former industrial lands) for more productive uses.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF19 develops a Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan for bioenergy and low-carbon products, including sustainability guidelines, feedstock definitions, incentives, and pathway identification to support emissions limits and avoid localized pollution.
subs.extraction_rationale: Clear definitional and evaluative action under AF19.

subs.policy_statement: Establish preferential pricing to prioritize residue feedstocks (e.g., low-grade wood, residues, waste materials) and prioritize lowest life-cycle GHG emissions feedstocks/products for incentives, considering impacts on other sectors.
subs.verbatim_text: - Incentivize residue feedstock use: NYSERDA, AGM, and DEC should work with SUNY ESF and Cornell CALS to establish preferential pricing to prioritize use of feedstocks that are residues from existing agricultural, forest, and waste systems (e.g., low-grade wood, wood residues, waste materials, and processing wastes). Feedstocks and products with the lowest GHG emissions following life cycle GHG accounting and analysis should be prioritized for incentives.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF19 develops a Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan for bioenergy and low-carbon products, including sustainability guidelines, feedstock definitions, incentives, and pathway identification to support emissions limits and avoid localized pollution.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific incentive/pricing mechanism under AF19.

subs.policy_statement: Develop energy systems supporting a net zero emissions economy, including programs leveraging private capital to invest in conversion technology for bio-based feedstocks into bio-based products.
subs.verbatim_text: - Develop net zero energy systems: NYSERDA and DPS should develop energy systems that can best support a net zero emissions economy in New York, including programs that leverage private capital to invest in conversion technology for bio-based feedstock into bio-based products.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF19 develops a Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan for bioenergy and low-carbon products, including sustainability guidelines, feedstock definitions, incentives, and pathway identification to support emissions limits and avoid localized pollution.
subs.extraction_rationale: Program development and investment leverage action under AF19.

subs.policy_statement: Identify bioenergy pathways with high life-cycle energy efficiency and emissions reductions that complement electrification, and develop alternative fuel strategies for hard-to-electrify applications.
subs.verbatim_text: - Identify efficient bioenergy pathways: NYSERDA should work with Cornell CALS and SUNY ESF to identify bioenergy pathways with high life cycle energy efficiency and high emissions reductions (from feedstock production, conversion, and delivery to the end user) that complement and support wide-scale electrification. NYSERDA and DEC should develop alternative fuel strategies for challenging to electrify applications.
subs.parent_policy_name: AF19 develops a Sustainable Biomass Feedstock Action Plan for bioenergy and low-carbon products, including sustainability guidelines, feedstock definitions, incentives, and pathway identification to support emissions limits and avoid localized pollution.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific pathway identification and alternative fuel strategy development under AF19.

subs.policy_statement: Identify 2050 hard-to-decarbonize fuel needs (e.g., jet fuels) and potential bioenergy development to meet these needs, tracking federal developments.
subs.verbatim_text: - Address hard to decarbonize fuel needs: NYSERDA and DPS should work with SUNY ESF and Cornell CALS to identify 2050 hard-to-decarbonize fuel needs (such as high-quality distillate jet fuels) and potential bioenergy development (feedstock supply chain, conversion systems, and end use markets) to meet these needs. The State should continue to follow developments on the federal level and be positioned to capture opportunities related to these future needs.

parent.verbatim_text: Reducing the disposal of organics, including food scraps... is key to reducing the methane and CO2 emissions at landfills and combustion facilities.

subs.verbatim_text:
- Significantly reduce the disposal of organics: The State should enact legislation to amend and expand the existing Food Donation and Food Scraps Recycling Law (2019) to include generators of one ton per week or more beginning in 2024, one-half ton per week in 2025, and all generators
- by 2028. Also, the law should be amended immediately to eliminate the 25-mile requirement and the exemptions.
