section_header: w3. extended producer responsibility/product stewardship

parent.policy_statement: W3 enacts extended producer responsibility (EPR)/product stewardship requirements for end-of-life management of post-consumer products, including a 2023 framework and amendments to existing product stewardship laws.
parent.verbatim_text: The CJWG is strongly supportive of policies focused on waste reduction and has expressed support for EPR, indicating that passage of an EPR bill should be a priority for addressing emissions from the waste sector.
parent.section_header: W3. Extended Producer Responsibility/Product Stewardship
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with explicit components requiring enactment and amendments to laws/regulations.

subs.policy_statement: Enact and implement new EPR/product stewardship framework legislation in 2023 or enact individual EPR laws targeting high-GHG-impact products.
subs.verbatim_text: - EPR framework legislation: The State should enact and implement new legislation in 2023 that creates an EPR/product stewardship framework. Alternatively, individual legislation should be enacted targeting products with the greatest GHG impact (such as packaging and printed paper, carpet, tires, textiles, solar panels, wind turbines, batteries, appliances, especially those containing refrigerants, and mattresses).
subs.parent_policy_name: W3 enacts extended producer responsibility (EPR)/product stewardship requirements for end-of-life management of post-consumer products, including a 2023 framework and amendments to existing product stewardship laws.
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit 2023 legislative commitment with scope options under W3.

subs.policy_statement: Amend the Electronic Equipment Recycling and Reuse Act to improve e-waste performance (e.g., shift from target-based collection to consumer convenience) and provide DEC outreach on manufacturer requirements.
subs.verbatim_text: - Electronic Equipment Recycling and Reuse Act: The State should enact legislation to amend the Electronic Equipment Recycling and Reuse Act to improve overall program performance for e-waste by, for example, moving away from a target-based collection approach to a consumer convenience model. DEC should provide outreach to the regulated community and consumers regarding the manufacturers' requirements.
subs.parent_policy_name: W3 enacts extended producer responsibility (EPR)/product stewardship requirements for end-of-life management of post-consumer products, including a 2023 framework and amendments to existing product stewardship laws.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific statutory amendment and outreach action under W3.

subs.policy_statement: Amend the Rechargeable Battery Law to require collection/recycling of additional battery types and increase DEC compliance monitoring and enforcement.
subs.verbatim_text: - Rechargeable Battery Recycling Law: The State should enact legislation to amend to the Rechargeable Battery Law to require the collection and recycling of additional consumer battery types (alkaline, electric and hybrid vehicle batteries, etc.). DEC should increase program compliance monitoring and enforcement in accordance with existing statute to improve manufacturer engagement, retailer participation and consumer convenience.
subs.parent_policy_name: W3 enacts extended producer responsibility (EPR)/product stewardship requirements for end-of-life management of post-consumer products, including a 2023 framework and amendments to existing product stewardship laws.
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit amendment and enforcement actions under W3.

subs.policy_statement: Amend the Mercury Thermostat Collection Act to extend the program beyond the January 1, 2024 sunset date and improve performance.
subs.verbatim_text: - Mercury Thermostat Collection Act: The State should enact legislation to amend the Mercury Thermostat Collection Act to extend the program beyond the current January 1, 2024, sunset date and to improve overall program performance.
subs.parent_policy_name: W3 enacts extended producer responsibility (EPR)/product stewardship requirements for end-of-life management of post-consumer products, including a 2023 framework and amendments to existing product stewardship laws.
subs.extraction_rationale: Statutory amendment with explicit date under W3.

subs.policy_statement: Amend Universal Waste (6 NYCRR Part 373) and Part 360 series regulations to streamline post-consumer paint management.
subs.verbatim_text: - Post-Consumer Paint Collection Program: DEC should amend the 6 NYCRR Part 373 Universal Waste regulations and the 6 NYCRR Part 360 series regulation to help streamline the management of post-consumer paint in New York.
subs.parent_policy_name: W3 enacts extended producer responsibility (EPR)/product stewardship requirements for end-of-life management of post-consumer products, including a 2023 framework and amendments to existing product stewardship laws.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific regulatory amendments under W3.

section_header: w4. water resource recovery facility conversion

parent.policy_statement: W4 converts wastewater treatment plants to WRRFs emphasizing beneficial product capture, supporting beneficial use, anaerobic digestion optimization, and co-pollutant research.
parent.verbatim_text: Transforming wastewater treatment plants from a waste disposal priority to WRRFs that emphasize the capture of beneficial products is a key component of the circular economy.
parent.section_header: W4. Water Resource Recovery Facility Conversion
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with components listing State support, incentives, and evaluation actions.

subs.policy_statement: Support beneficial use of biosolids and biogas with air quality/health/GHG benefit requirements and avoid localized pollution in Disadvantaged Communities; incentivize WRRF self-supply of energy needs where it provides electric system capacity for electrification.
subs.verbatim_text: - Beneficial use: The State should support beneficial use of biosolids and biogas that demonstrate air quality, health, and GHG benefits, including requirements to avoid localized pollution in Disadvantaged Communities... Self-supply of energy needs at WRRFs should be incentivized, particularly when it provides electric system capacity for electrification of transportation and buildings with a focus on Disadvantaged Communities.
subs.parent_policy_name: W4 converts wastewater treatment plants to WRRFs emphasizing beneficial product capture, supporting beneficial use, anaerobic digestion optimization, and co-pollutant research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific support and incentive actions with equity safeguards under W4.

subs.policy_statement: Support energy production and methane mitigation from anaerobic digestion using full life-cycle analysis, require methane leakage measurement/abatement, support co-digestion using existing capacity, and require best-practice/low-emissions system designs/retrofits.
subs.verbatim_text: - Optimize and expand anerobic digestion: The State should support energy production and methane mitigation following a full life cycle analysis, including measurement and abatement of methane leakage... Programs that incentivize anerobic digestion should require systems be built (or retrofit) for maximum methane mitigation to ensure development of well-managed, low emissions biogas or RNG production...
subs.parent_policy_name: W4 converts wastewater treatment plants to WRRFs emphasizing beneficial product capture, supporting beneficial use, anaerobic digestion optimization, and co-pollutant research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Detailed conditions and requirements for AD incentives under W4.

subs.policy_statement: Evaluate co-pollutants (e.g., emerging contaminants) and advance control technologies to reduce or eliminate these emissions from WRRF-related processes.
subs.verbatim_text: - Research co-pollutants: The State should evaluate the extent and impact of co-pollutants such as emerging contaminants and advance the use of control technologies to reduce or eliminate these emissions.
subs.parent_policy_name: W4 converts wastewater treatment plants to WRRFs emphasizing beneficial product capture, supporting beneficial use, anaerobic digestion optimization, and co-pollutant research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit evaluation and control-technology advancement under W4.

section_header: w5. refrigerant leak reduction and destruction

parent.policy_statement: W5 reduces refrigerant leaks and ensures destruction at end-of-life through DEC regulations, EPR legislation, sales bans for virgin high-GWP servicing refrigerants, reporting registries, and research.
parent.verbatim_text: Policies incentivizing the destruction of refrigerants at end-of-life would encourage the retirement of old, energy-inefficient equipment so that these materials are not released to the atmosphere.
parent.section_header: W5. Refrigerant Leak Reduction and Destruction
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with components specifying regulations, legislation, registries, and research.

subs.policy_statement: Promulgate regulations requiring reclamation or destruction of refrigerants from appliances at end-of-life with verification and reporting; enact EPR legislation for refrigerant-containing appliances.
subs.verbatim_text: - End of Life: DEC should promulgate regulations to address leaks at end-of-life by requiring reclamation or destruction of refrigerants from appliances and institute requirements for verification and reporting. The State should enact EPR legislation for refrigerant-containing appliances.
subs.parent_policy_name: W5 reduces refrigerant leaks and ensures destruction at end-of-life through DEC regulations, EPR legislation, sales bans for virgin high-GWP servicing refrigerants, reporting registries, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Regulatory requirement plus EPR legislation under W5.

subs.policy_statement: Promulgate regulations banning sale of virgin high-GWP refrigerants for servicing, with an exception for reclaimed refrigerants.
subs.verbatim_text: - Appliance Servicing: DEC should promulgate regulations banning the sale of virgin high GWP refrigerants for servicing with an exception for reclaimed refrigerants.
subs.parent_policy_name: W5 reduces refrigerant leaks and ensures destruction at end-of-life through DEC regulations, EPR legislation, sales bans for virgin high-GWP servicing refrigerants, reporting registries, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific sales ban regulation under W5.

subs.policy_statement: Create a registry and reporting requirements to track sales, stockpiles, and leaks for large refrigeration/HVAC systems and refrigerant wholesalers/distributors.
subs.verbatim_text: - Reporting: DEC should create a registry and reporting requirements (to track sales, stockpiles, and leaks) for large refrigeration and heating, ventilation, and air conditioning (HVAC) systems and refrigerant wholesalers and distributors.
subs.parent_policy_name: W5 reduces refrigerant leaks and ensures destruction at end-of-life through DEC regulations, EPR legislation, sales bans for virgin high-GWP servicing refrigerants, reporting registries, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Registry/reporting system is a concrete policy tool under W5.

subs.policy_statement: Research end-of-life management approaches for various refrigerants and alternatives.
subs.verbatim_text: - Research: DEC should research end-of-life management for various refrigerants and their alternatives.
subs.parent_policy_name: W5 reduces refrigerant leaks and ensures destruction at end-of-life through DEC regulations, EPR legislation, sales bans for virgin high-GWP servicing refrigerants, reporting registries, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Research commitment under W5.

section_header: w6. reduce fugitive emissions of methane and co-pollutants from solid waste management facilities

parent.policy_statement: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
parent.verbatim_text: Identifying and reducing fugitive emissions of methane from landfills and anaerobic digesters through baseline measurement, increased monitoring, and engineering and regulatory programs can reduce leaks and provide facilities with comprehensive data on their operations.
parent.section_header: W6. Reduce Fugitive Emissions of Methane and Co-Pollutants from Solid Waste Management Facilities
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with a components list including regulatory changes, monitoring mandates, and funding.

subs.policy_statement: Enhance landfill regulations before 2025 to reduce methane leakage (earlier gas collection installation, specialty collectors, improved dewatering).
subs.verbatim_text: - Landfill gas capture for active landfills: DEC should enhance existing regulations, before 2025, for landfills that will lead to less methane leakage, such as a requirement to install gas collection systems sooner after waste placement, install specialty landfill gas collectors for difficult to access areas, and enhance gas dewatering systems to increase collection efficiency.
subs.parent_policy_name: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific regulatory enhancement with timeline (before 2025) under W6.

subs.policy_statement: Provide additional funding for the municipal landfill closure and landfill gas management grant program to control emissions from closed landfills.
subs.verbatim_text: - Control emissions from closed landfills: The State should provide additional funding for the municipal landfill closure and landfill gas management grant program.
subs.parent_policy_name: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Funding expansion commitment under W6.

subs.policy_statement: Develop regulations for enhanced landfill covers to increase methane oxidation.
subs.verbatim_text: - Enhanced landfill cover systems: DEC should develop regulations for enhanced landfill covers to increase oxidation of methane.
subs.parent_policy_name: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Regulatory development action under W6.

subs.policy_statement: Require improved emissions monitoring at applicable solid waste facilities using new technologies (e.g., drones) and require emissions reductions at these facilities.
subs.verbatim_text: - Emission monitoring and emission reduction: DEC should require improved emissions monitoring programs at all applicable solid waste management facilities, utilizing new technologies (such as drones). Also, DEC should require reductions in emissions at these facilities.
subs.parent_policy_name: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Monitoring mandate plus reduction requirement under W6.

subs.policy_statement: Provide funding for proper closure of municipal landfills to reduce emissions.
subs.verbatim_text: - Funding to aid municipal landfills:  he State should provide funding for the proper closure of municipal landfills and reduce emissions.
subs.parent_policy_name: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Funding commitment under W6.

subs.policy_statement: Research landfill and anaerobic digestion emissions to evaluate monitoring techniques, quantify fugitive emissions, and evaluate best gas uses during electrification transition; research regulatory frameworks/best practices to minimize methane and co-pollutant emissions from AD/RNG.
subs.verbatim_text: - Research: DEC should research comprehensive landfill GHG emissions to evaluate monitoring techniques, quantify fugitive GHG emissions, and evaluate most appropriate uses for the gas during the transition to statewide electrification. DEC should research methods to improve estimates of methane emissions from anaerobic digestion and strategies to reduce or eliminate these emissions, including consideration of a regulatory framework to ensure best practices to minimize methane and co-pollutant emissions from anerobic digestion and RNG production.
subs.parent_policy_name: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit research program including regulatory framework consideration under W6.

subs.policy_statement: Use regulations, monitoring, and enforcement to reduce co-pollutant impacts from solid waste management facilities and enact legislation to reduce toxics in consumer products and support EPR to reduce co-pollutants.
subs.verbatim_text: - Co-pollutant control: DEC should use regulations, monitoring, and enforcement to reduce the impact from co-pollutants associated with solid waste management facilities. The State should enact legislation to support reduction of toxic substances in consumer products, as well as EPR (see Strategy W2 and Strategy W3), since these efforts will also reduce the potential for copollutants present at solid waste management facilities.
subs.parent_policy_name: W6 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutant emissions from solid waste management facilities through enhanced landfill regulations, monitoring requirements, funding for closure/management, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific control mechanisms plus legislative action referenced under W6.

section_header: w7. reduce fugitive emissions of methane and co-pollutants from water resource recovery facilities

parent.policy_statement: W7 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutants from WRRFs through biogas capture/use, monitoring and leak reduction, septic maintenance funding mechanism, sewer transition assistance, operator training, and research.
parent.verbatim_text: Municipalities will need to measure and reduce GHG emissions from WRRFs, septic, and sewer systems, which can be difficult without the proper equipment and training.
parent.section_header: W7. Reduce Fugitive Emissions of Methane and Co-Pollutants from Water Resource Recovery Facilities
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with a component list including regulatory, funding, training, and research actions.

subs.policy_statement: Repair and consistently operate WRRF biogas equipment and identify strategic beneficial uses of captured biogas before flaring excess capacity.
subs.verbatim_text: - Capture and beneficially reuse fugitive biogas: DEC, EFC, and NYSERDA should work with local utilities and municipalities to repair and consistently operate WRRF flares, boilers, engines, or other equipment on-site and identify strategic beneficial uses of captured biogas before flaring excess capacity (see Strategy W9).
subs.parent_policy_name: W7 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutants from WRRFs through biogas capture/use, monitoring and leak reduction, septic maintenance funding mechanism, sewer transition assistance, operator training, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Operational repair and beneficial use planning under W7.

subs.policy_statement: Require emissions monitoring and leak reduction at WRRFs.
subs.verbatim_text: - Monitoring: DEC should require emissions monitoring and leak reduction at WRRFs.
subs.parent_policy_name: W7 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutants from WRRFs through biogas capture/use, monitoring and leak reduction, septic maintenance funding mechanism, sewer transition assistance, operator training, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Clear regulatory requirement under W7.

subs.policy_statement: Enact legislation establishing a municipal funding mechanism for routine septic system maintenance via contractual services.
subs.verbatim_text: - Ensure proper maintenance of septic systems at the municipal level: The State should enact legislation to establish a municipal funding mechanism to allow contractual services for routine maintenance on septic systems.
subs.parent_policy_name: W7 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutants from WRRFs through biogas capture/use, monitoring and leak reduction, septic maintenance funding mechanism, sewer transition assistance, operator training, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Legislative funding mechanism under W7.

subs.policy_statement: Provide financial assistance for sewer hookups to defray high upfront sewering costs.
subs.verbatim_text: - Encouraging transition to sewer: The State should provide financial assistance for sewer hookups to defray high upfront costs of sewering.
subs.parent_policy_name: W7 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutants from WRRFs through biogas capture/use, monitoring and leak reduction, septic maintenance funding mechanism, sewer transition assistance, operator training, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Financial assistance commitment under W7.

subs.policy_statement: Provide financial support and job training to wastewater system operators.
subs.verbatim_text: - Operator training: The State should provide financial support and job training to wastewater system operators.
subs.parent_policy_name: W7 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutants from WRRFs through biogas capture/use, monitoring and leak reduction, septic maintenance funding mechanism, sewer transition assistance, operator training, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Workforce training and funding under W7.

subs.policy_statement: Support research on WRRF emissions profiles and how emissions can be controlled.
subs.verbatim_text: - Research: DEC should support research on the emission profile of WRRFs and how they can be controlled.
subs.parent_policy_name: W7 reduces fugitive methane and co-pollutants from WRRFs through biogas capture/use, monitoring and leak reduction, septic maintenance funding mechanism, sewer transition assistance, operator training, and research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit research support under W7.

section_header: w8. recycling markets

parent.policy_statement: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
parent.verbatim_text: Implementing additional funding resources for municipalities and implementing sound post-consumer content requirements across different sectors can make secondary material processing cost competitive with virgin materials...
parent.section_header: W8. Recycling Markets
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with components including legislation, procurement, tax credits, and studies.

subs.policy_statement: Support domestic recycling facilities/markets and enact recycled content requirements for certain products and packaging; incentivize public-private partnerships for recycling facility development.
subs.verbatim_text: - Market development for recovered resources: The State should support domestic recycling facilities and markets for recovered resources (including compost, digestate, and recycled aggregate/building deconstruction materials) and incentivize public-private partnerships for recycling facility development. The State should also enact legislation to require a minimum level of recycled content in certain products and packaging.
subs.parent_policy_name: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit market support plus legislative recycled-content requirement under W8.

subs.policy_statement: Provide financial assistance to research increased capture and reuse of building deconstruction materials and recovered aggregate, including potential government procurement/bid requirements.
subs.verbatim_text: - Reuse of building materials: The State should provide financial assistance to research the increase of capture and reuse of building deconstruction materials and recovered aggregate. This may include government requirements (such as procurement standards and bid specifications) to include recycled or reused deconstruction materials.
subs.parent_policy_name: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
subs.extraction_rationale: Financial assistance for research and potential procurement requirements under W8.

subs.policy_statement: Enhance and implement green procurement programs requiring use of recyclables (e.g., compost, construction aggregate) by State/local entities and contractors.
subs.verbatim_text: - Recyclables in green procurement: DEC and OGS should enhance and implement new green procurement programs to require the use of recyclables (such as compost and construction aggregate) by State and local entities and those contracting with the government.
subs.parent_policy_name: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
subs.extraction_rationale: Procurement requirement under W8.

subs.policy_statement: Enact a production tax credit for companies that turn recycled materials into intermediate products if they locate facilities in New York.
subs.verbatim_text: - Production tax credit for recycled products: The State should enact a production tax credit, similar to the credit for clean energy systems, that is applied to companies that turn recycled materials into intermediate products if they locate their facilities in New York.
subs.parent_policy_name: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit tax credit legislation under W8.

subs.policy_statement: Conduct an organics market study (organics roadmap) on quantities/characteristics and end uses of organics statewide.
subs.verbatim_text: - Organics roadmap: DEC should conduct a market study of the quantity and characteristics of organics (food waste, biosolids, other high strength waste) produced statewide, including possible end uses of such products (such as agriculture, mine reclamation, roadside soil amendments, recycled bioproducts such as bioplastics, and erosion control).
subs.parent_policy_name: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific statewide study commitment under W8.

subs.policy_statement: Continue financial support to NY colleges/universities for research on recycling market challenges (plastics, low-grade paper, glass innovations).
subs.verbatim_text: - Research on markets: The State should continue financial support of colleges and universities within New York in researching recycling market challenges, plastics recycling, low-grade paper recycling, and glass processing innovations for New York.
subs.parent_policy_name: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
subs.extraction_rationale: Research funding support under W8.

subs.policy_statement: Increase State purchasing of products with recycled content and recycled products (e.g., compost).
subs.verbatim_text: - State purchasing: State agencies, authorities, and local governments should support greater purchasing of products with recycled content as well as the purchase of recycled products (compost, etc.).
subs.parent_policy_name: W8 develops recycling markets by supporting domestic recycling facilities and markets, recycled content requirements, green procurement, tax credits, organics market studies, research support, and State purchasing.
subs.extraction_rationale: Procurement behavior commitment under W8.

section_header: w9. biogas use

parent.policy_statement: W9 supports strategic biogas use during the transition by evaluating local uses with air quality/health/GHG safeguards, developing pricing models and funding for organics infrastructure, and conducting comprehensive emissions research.
parent.verbatim_text: The limited supply of biogas or RNG should be targeted to strategic uses such as locations where it can provide electric system capacity for buildings and transportation electrification by alleviating system constraints.
parent.section_header: W9. Biogas Use
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with components specifying evaluation, pricing models, funding mechanisms, and research studies.

subs.policy_statement: Evaluate strategic and local uses of biogas-derived fuels/electricity/energy stressing fuel cells, waste transport fuels, co-location/cogeneration, hard-to-electrify uses, and requiring demonstration of air quality/health/GHG benefits and avoiding localized pollution in Disadvantaged Communities.
subs.verbatim_text: - Strategic use of biogas: NYSERDA and New York State Department of Public Service (DPS), along with utilities, should evaluate strategic and local uses of generated fuels, electricity, or other energy produced from biogas for essential needs during the transition to electrification and other low-emissions energy sources. This evaluation should stress the use of fuel cells for electricity in lieu of generators or pipeline use... This evaluation should demonstrate air quality, health, and GHG benefits before implementation, including requirements to avoid localized pollution in Disadvantaged Communities.
subs.parent_policy_name: W9 supports strategic biogas use during the transition by evaluating local uses with air quality/health/GHG safeguards, developing pricing models and funding for organics infrastructure, and conducting comprehensive emissions research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Detailed evaluation requirement and safeguards under W9.

subs.policy_statement: Identify energy pricing models and conduct a market-based study for waste-generated biogas; require AD incentive programs to use maximum methane mitigation system designs/retrofits; provide a funding mechanism to support organics recycling infrastructure.
subs.verbatim_text: - Funding for organics recycling infrastructure: NYSERDA and DPS, along with utilities, should identify energy pricing models and conduct a market-based study for waste-generated biogas. Programs that incentivize anerobic digestion should require systems be built (or retrofit) for maximum methane mitigation... The State should also provide a funding mechanism to support an organics recycling infrastructure.
subs.parent_policy_name: W9 supports strategic biogas use during the transition by evaluating local uses with air quality/health/GHG safeguards, developing pricing models and funding for organics infrastructure, and conducting comprehensive emissions research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Pricing model study plus conditional incentive design and infrastructure funding mechanism under W9.

subs.policy_statement: Complete a comprehensive landfill gas and WRRF emissions research study to evaluate monitoring techniques, quantify fugitive emissions, evaluate appropriate gas uses, and improve AD emission estimates and mitigation methods.
subs.verbatim_text: - Research: DEC should complete a comprehensive landfill gas and WRRF emissions research study. The study should evaluate emissions monitoring techniques, quantify fugitive emissions, and evaluate the most appropriate uses for the gas during the transition to statewide electrification.
subs.parent_policy_name: W9 supports strategic biogas use during the transition by evaluating local uses with air quality/health/GHG safeguards, developing pricing models and funding for organics infrastructure, and conducting comprehensive emissions research.
subs.extraction_rationale: Research study commitment under W9.

section_header: lu1. mitigate carbon emissions by protection of forest lands

parent.policy_statement: LU1 protects forest lands to mitigate carbon emissions via legislation, enhanced land acquisition/easement programs, increased funding, and a statewide target to acquire 500,000 forested acres by 2050.
parent.verbatim_text: The State should implement the following tactics that keep forests as forests and maintain New York's forest carbon sequestration and storage levels and prevent emissions from development.
parent.section_header: LU1. Mitigate Carbon Emissions by Protection of Forest Lands
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with a components list including legislation and quantified acquisition goal by 2050.

subs.policy_statement: Immediately enact a 'Keep Forests as Forests' law requiring developers to purchase and set aside forested land when forest carbon is lost during development, following avoid/minimize/mitigate principles.
subs.verbatim_text: - Enact 'Keep Forests as Forests' law: The State should immediately enact legislation to 'keep forest as forests,' requiring developers to purchase and set aside forested land when forest carbon is lost during development following the principles of avoid, minimize, and mitigate.
subs.parent_policy_name: LU1 protects forest lands to mitigate carbon emissions via legislation, enhanced land acquisition/easement programs, increased funding, and a statewide target to acquire 500,000 forested acres by 2050.
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit immediate legislation with a defined developer requirement under LU1.

subs.policy_statement: Enhance support for local land acquisition and conservation easements through programs such as the Community Preservation Act, Conservation Partnership Program, Forest Conservation Easements for Land Trusts, and Community Forest programs.
subs.verbatim_text: - Establish programs to support local land acquisition: DEC should considerably enhance support for local land acquisition and conservation easements by municipalities and land trusts through mechanisms such as the Community Preservation Act, Conservation Partnership Program, Forest Conservation Easements for Land Trusts, and Community Forest programs.
subs.parent_policy_name: LU1 protects forest lands to mitigate carbon emissions via legislation, enhanced land acquisition/easement programs, increased funding, and a statewide target to acquire 500,000 forested acres by 2050.
subs.extraction_rationale: Program support expansion under LU1 with named mechanisms.

subs.policy_statement: Maintain and significantly increase land acquisition funding (fee and easements) by State agencies, municipalities, and land trusts.
subs.verbatim_text: - Maintain and increase State land acquisition: DEC should continue to maintain and significantly increase land acquisition (fee and conservation easement) funding by State agencies, municipalities, and land trusts.
subs.parent_policy_name: LU1 protects forest lands to mitigate carbon emissions via legislation, enhanced land acquisition/easement programs, increased funding, and a statewide target to acquire 500,000 forested acres by 2050.
subs.extraction_rationale: Funding increase commitment under LU1.

subs.policy_statement: Collaboratively aim to acquire at least 500,000 forested acres by 2050, prioritizing highest carbon/climate/conservation benefit forests.
subs.verbatim_text: - Increase collaborative efforts for statewide forest acquisition: Statewide agencies and organizations, land trusts, programs, and local municipalities should collaboratively aim to expand upon historical land acquisition rates to acquire at least 500,000 forested acres by 2050, prioritizing forests with the highest quality carbon, climate, and other conservation benefits.
subs.parent_policy_name: LU1 protects forest lands to mitigate carbon emissions via legislation, enhanced land acquisition/easement programs, increased funding, and a statewide target to acquire 500,000 forested acres by 2050.
subs.extraction_rationale: Quantified acreage target with deadline (2050) under LU1.

section_header: lu2. afforestation and reforestation

parent.policy_statement: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
parent.verbatim_text: In order to ensure that reforestation and afforestation are effective GHG emissions mitigation strategies by 2030 and 2050, these efforts need to be started as soon as possible to allow time for seedling establishment.
parent.section_header: LU2. Afforestation and Reforestation
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with extensive components including quantified acreage/tree targets and program creation.

subs.policy_statement: Develop a Statewide Reforestation and Afforestation Plan with goals for 2030/2040/2050 and encourage/support programs to establish and maintain 1.7 million acres (~680 million trees) by 2040.
subs.verbatim_text: Develop a Statewide Reforestation and Afforestation Plan: DEC should work with other agencies and partners to develop a plan that sets ambitious and collaborative goals... for reforestation and afforestation by 2030, 2040, and 2050. State agencies, authorities, partners, and organizations should broadly encourage and support statewide tree planting, tree regeneration, and tree maintenance programs to establish and maintain 1.7 million acres (approximately 680 million trees) by 2040.
subs.parent_policy_name: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit plan creation with deadlines and quantified acreage/tree target under LU2.

subs.policy_statement: Identify and prioritize locations where afforestation/reforestation are most likely to succeed using data and expert input.
subs.verbatim_text: - Prioritize locations for afforestation and reforestation: DEC should identify areas where afforestation and reforestation are the most likely to succeed using data provided by the Reforestation Hub, experts, and other authorities.
subs.parent_policy_name: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific siting prioritization action under LU2.

subs.policy_statement: Develop partnerships and programs to reforest State rights-of-way (roadsides, pipelines, railroads, utility corridors) using compatible species and 'right tree, right place' outreach.
subs.verbatim_text: - Reforest rights-of-way: DOT should work with public and private partners on reforestation efforts in right-of-way areas of the State... and develop programs for afforestation and reforestation in these locations. Public outreach for right tree, right place is needed.
subs.parent_policy_name: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.

subs.verbatim_text: - Invest in and update the Colonel William F. Fox Memorial Saratoga Tree Nursery: The State should provide funding to increase the State tree nursery's staff and infrastructure capacity to support large-scale afforestation and reforestation efforts... and implementing upgrades to enhance seed collection, seed storage, seedling production, workforce development, and pre- and postplanting practices.
subs.parent_policy_name: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit funding and capacity expansion at a named facility under LU2.

subs.policy_statement: Create a statewide program/network to increase tree seed collection and efficiently deliver seeds to the State nursery for processing, storage, and use.
subs.verbatim_text: - Increase and accelerate seed collection: DEC should create a program to increase statewide tree seed collection across the state. This program should establish a network of seed collectors and an efficient method to deliver seeds to the Colonel William F. Fox Memorial Saratoga Tree Nursery for processing, storage, and use.
subs.parent_policy_name: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.

The State should also increase funding opportunities such as Regenerate NY or the ReLeaf program's Community Canopy program for private individuals to establish and maintain privately owned trees.
subs.parent_policy_name: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
subs.extraction_rationale: Funding expansion and named programs under LU2.

subs.policy_statement: Develop an urban tree planting opportunity assessment to focus establishment/maintenance where greatest climate/societal/health benefits occur.
subs.verbatim_text: - Prioritize locations for urban forest tree planting: ... DEC should develop an opportunity assessment to focus tree establishment and maintenance efforts within urban areas and communities where the most climate, societal, and public health benefits are likely to be achieved.
subs.parent_policy_name: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific assessment development action under LU2.

This may include sharing resources (like equipment, staff, and bulk ordering).
subs.parent_policy_name: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
subs.extraction_rationale: Guidance/support/funding actions under LU2.

subs.policy_statement: Expand funding for cost-share programs (e.g., Regenerate NY) to help landowners implement reforestation/afforestation while larger initiatives are established.
subs.verbatim_text: - Fund cost-share programs: The State should continue to expand the funding for cost share programs, such as Regenerate NY, to assist forest landowners in widespread implementation of reforestation and afforestation efforts.
subs.parent_policy_name: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit cost-share funding expansion under LU2.

subs.policy_statement: Develop a tree planting equipment loan program to provide access to specialized equipment for small- and large-scale planting projects.

subs.parent_policy_name: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.

subs.parent_policy_name: LU2 advances afforestation and reforestation through a statewide plan with acreage/tree targets, right-of-way reforestation, nursery investment, seed collection, grants, cost-share, equipment loans, free seedlings, and creation of a NY Tree/Climate Corps.

section_header: lg1. establish community greenhouse gas dashboard

parent.policy_statement: Establish Community Greenhouse Gas Dashboard (LG1): develop a statewide dashboard of community GHG emissions inventories to support local planning, equity monitoring, progress measurement, and data consistency.
parent.verbatim_text:
## LG1.  Establish Community Greenhouse Gas Dashboard

One strategy for supporting local climate action is to develop a statewide dashboard of community GHG emissions inventories to promote local climate action planning, monitor equity considerations, measure progress, and ensure data consistency at the county and municipal levels.
parent.section_header: LG1.  Establish Community Greenhouse Gas Dashboard
parent.extraction_rationale: Creates a specific statewide instrument (dashboard) with defined purpose for tracking/monitoring emissions and equity.

subs.policy_statement: Establish a Community GHG Working Group led by NYSERDA and DEC to develop/standardize local community GHG emissions inventories and secure New York-specific data.
subs.verbatim_text:
- Form a community GHG working group: NYSERDA and DEC should establish a Community GHG Working Group consisting, as appropriate, of Metropolitan Planning Organizations (MPOs), utilities, State agencies, academic institutions, consultants, labor representation, community benefit organizations, and regional and municipal officials. The group may consider several activities:

- Review existing guidance including the International Council for Local Environmental Initiative's U.S. Community Protocol for Accounting and Reporting of Greenhouse Gas Emissions to identify methods
- Identify and secure New York-specific data needed to complete the emission inventories
- Develop standards for local community GHG emissions inventories
subs.parent_policy_name: Establish Community Greenhouse Gas Dashboard (LG1): develop a statewide dashboard of community GHG emissions inventories to support local planning, equity monitoring, progress measurement, and data consistency.
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete institutional action (establish working group) with defined tasks.

subs.policy_statement: Incentivize development of software/tools to modernize GHG emissions accounting for local climate action, consistent with Climate Act methodology.
subs.verbatim_text: - Support modernization of GHG emission accounting to facilitate data accessibility: NYSERDA should incentivize software developers to create new software and other tools that allow for clear and transparent accounting of GHG emissions to inform and support local climate action. These tools should include considerations for the emissions accounting methodology required by the Climate Act.
subs.parent_policy_name: Establish Community Greenhouse Gas Dashboard (LG1): develop a statewide dashboard of community GHG emissions inventories to support local planning, equity monitoring, progress measurement, and data consistency.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (incentivize software developers) to create tools supporting accounting.

subs.policy_statement: Launch and maintain the community GHG dashboard with accessible, secure, privacy-protecting inventory information.
subs.verbatim_text: - Launch the community dashboard: NYSERDA, working with DEC and community stakeholders, should launch and maintain the dashboard, ensuring that GHG emissions inventory information is accessible to all communities in a manner that ensures data security and protects privacy.
subs.parent_policy_name: Establish Community Greenhouse Gas Dashboard (LG1): develop a statewide dashboard of community GHG emissions inventories to support local planning, equity monitoring, progress measurement, and data consistency.
subs.extraction_rationale: Action commitment to launch and maintain a statewide dashboard with specified safeguards.

section_header: lg2. develop local energy policies

parent.policy_statement: Develop Local Energy Policies (LG2): support local governments to demonstrate energy efficiency leadership through model above-minimum energy codes and construction policies (including NYStretch Energy Code, enhanced enforcement, and PACE).
parent.verbatim_text:
## LG2. Develop Local Energy Policies

This strategy is intended to encourage and support local governments to demonstrate leadership in energy efficiency by developing model above-minimum energy conservation codes and construction policies. This includes adopting the NYStretch Energy Code and promoting its adoption, enhanced code enforcement including streamlined permitting, third-party inspections, and shared enforcement, and Property Assessed Clean Energy financing.
parent.section_header: LG2. Develop Local Energy Policies
parent.extraction_rationale: Defines a strategy with specific policy mechanisms (codes, enforcement, PACE) and has listed components.

subs.policy_statement: Continue updating Clean Energy Communities and Climate Smart Communities programs to encourage adoption of emerging local energy policies and proactive climate action.
subs.verbatim_text: - Leverage existing NYSERDA and DEC programs: NYSERDA and DEC should continue to update the Clean Energy Communities and Climate Smart Communities programs to encourage adoption of emerging local energy policies and pro-active climate action.
subs.parent_policy_name: Develop Local Energy Policies (LG2): support local governments to demonstrate energy efficiency leadership through model above-minimum energy codes and construction policies (including NYStretch Energy Code, enhanced enforcement, and PACE).
subs.extraction_rationale: Ongoing program commitment to update existing state programs to drive local policy adoption.

subs.policy_statement: Continue and expand NYPA Clean Energy Services program to reach more communities.
subs.verbatim_text: - Expand NYPA Clean Energy Services Program: New York Power Authority (NYPA) should continue the existing Clean Energy Services program and expand the program to reach more communities.
subs.parent_policy_name: Develop Local Energy Policies (LG2): support local governments to demonstrate energy efficiency leadership through model above-minimum energy codes and construction policies (including NYStretch Energy Code, enhanced enforcement, and PACE).
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit program continuation and expansion directive.

subs.policy_statement: Expand services offered by the regional coordinator network to strengthen assistance to local governments, increasing support to small, resource-constrained, and underserved communities.
subs.verbatim_text: - Expand the regional coordinator network : NYSERDA and DEC should expand the type of services offered by the regional coordinator network to enhance and strengthen assistance to local governments and related entities across a range of climate actions. This expansion should increase support to small, resource-constrained, and underserved communities.
subs.parent_policy_name: Develop Local Energy Policies (LG2): support local governments to demonstrate energy efficiency leadership through model above-minimum energy codes and construction policies (including NYStretch Energy Code, enhanced enforcement, and PACE).
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific capacity-building action targeted to underserved communities.

section_header: lg3. provide clean energy siting support for local governments

parent.policy_statement: Provide Clean Energy Siting Support for Local Governments (LG3) through model local laws and streamlined permitting for renewable energy and storage.
parent.verbatim_text:
## LG3. Provide Clean Energy Siting Support for Local Governments

This strategy facilitates clean energy siting through development and promotion of model local laws and streamlined permitting.
parent.section_header: LG3. Provide Clean Energy Siting Support for Local Governments
parent.extraction_rationale: Strategy states a concrete intervention (model laws, streamlined permitting) and has components.

subs.policy_statement: Develop and promote model local laws and development regulations for clean energy siting via Clean Energy Communities and Climate Smart Communities, minimizing impacts on forests/agricultural lands and considering co-location.
subs.verbatim_text: - Create model local laws and regulations: NYSERDA, DEC, and DOS should work with community stakeholders and the clean energy industry to develop and promote model local laws and development regulations through the Clean Energy Communities and Climate Smart Communities programs. These model local laws and development regulations should minimize the impact of clean energy siting on forests and agricultural lands, as well as consider colocational opportunities for agriculture and renewable energy generation.
subs.parent_policy_name: Provide Clean Energy Siting Support for Local Governments (LG3) through model local laws and streamlined permitting for renewable energy and storage.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific deliverable (model laws/regulations) with implementation channel and constraints.

subs.policy_statement: Within one year, promote local adoption of the New York State Solar Permit and other actions to streamline permitting for clean energy technologies including energy storage.
subs.verbatim_text: - Promote New York State Solar Permit Adoption: Within one year, NYSERDA and DOS should work with code enforcement officers to promote local adoption of the New York State Solar Permit and other local actions to streamline the permitting process for clean energy technologies, including energy storage, at a variety of scales.
subs.parent_policy_name: Provide Clean Energy Siting Support for Local Governments (LG3) through model local laws and streamlined permitting for renewable energy and storage.
subs.extraction_rationale: Time-bound action ('Within one year') with specific instrument (NY State Solar Permit).

section_header: lg4. promote municipal leadership to support clean energy adoption

parent.policy_statement: Promote Municipal Leadership to Support Clean Energy Adoption (LG4) by connecting consumers with clean energy products/services through programs such as CCA, microgrids, and community-scale campaigns.
parent.verbatim_text:
## LG4.  Promote Municipal Leadership to Support Clean Energy Adoption

This strategy connects homes, businesses, and community institutions with clean energy products and services through Community Choice Aggregation (CCA) programs, microgrids, district systems, and community-scale campaigns to encourage adoption of innovative technologies to generate savings and reduce GHG emissions for consumers in an equitable manner.
parent.section_header: LG4.  Promote Municipal Leadership to Support Clean Energy Adoption
parent.extraction_rationale: Defines a programmatic strategy with specified mechanisms and equity intent; has components.

subs.policy_statement: Promote community-scale campaigns to encourage adoption of clean technologies.
subs.verbatim_text: - Encourage the adoption of clean technologies: NYSERDA should work with community stakeholders to promote community-scale campaigns to encourage the adoption of clean technologies to generate value and savings for consumers.
subs.parent_policy_name: Promote Municipal Leadership to Support Clean Energy Adoption (LG4) by connecting consumers with clean energy products/services through programs such as CCA, microgrids, and community-scale campaigns.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific intervention (community-scale campaigns) with implementing agency (NYSERDA).

subs.policy_statement: Expand workforce development programs for the clean energy economy focused on training and job placement.
subs.verbatim_text: - Expand workforce development for the clean energy economy: NYSERDA, in collaboration with unions and the clean energy industry, should expand workforce development programs focused on training and job placement in clean energy and emerging technologies.
subs.parent_policy_name: Promote Municipal Leadership to Support Clean Energy Adoption (LG4) by connecting consumers with clean energy products/services through programs such as CCA, microgrids, and community-scale campaigns.
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to expand workforce development with partners and outcomes.

parent.verbatim_text:
## LG5.  Provide State Support and Guidance

This strategy discusses continuing and expanding program opportunities, incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and centralized procurement services to motivate local governments... This includes LED lighting, energy efficiency upgrades, heat pump projects, methane recovery for energy production from wastewater treatment and landfills, solar on municipal premises, and municipal and school district fleet electrification.

subs.parent_policy_name: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.

subs.verbatim_text: - Provide technical support for clean energy projects: NYSERDA should work with community stakeholders to provide technical support to help local governments and related public entities develop and implement clean energy projects.
subs.parent_policy_name: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.

Waste (Strategy W4 and Strategy W9).

subs.parent_policy_name: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific deliverable (tools/resources) enabling direct purchasing.

subs.policy_statement: Support electrification of municipal and school district fleets while increasing fleet-wide fuel economy.
subs.verbatim_text: - Support fleet electrification: NYSERDA and DEC should support electrification of municipal and school district fleets while increasing fleet-wide fuel economy.
subs.parent_policy_name: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete support action for fleet electrification.

subs.policy_statement: Provide policy guidance and financial support to municipalities adopting building electrification and energy efficiency policies informed by city decarbonization plans (e.g., Ithaca).
subs.verbatim_text: - Support building electrification: NYSERDA should provide policy guidance and financial support to municipalities that adopt building electrification and energy efficiency policies that incorporate insights from cities like Ithaca that have made ambitious commitments and developed innovative decarbonization plans.
subs.parent_policy_name: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.

subs.parent_policy_name: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.

subs.policy_statement: Encourage intermunicipal coordination and planning on cross-boundary issues and ease access to funding without penalizing Disadvantaged Communities lacking partnerships.

subs.parent_policy_name: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.

subs.policy_statement: Provide educational materials and training to local governments/public entities so they understand available resources and are prepared to receive funding.

subs.parent_policy_name: Provide State Support and Guidance (LG5): continue/expand incentives, technical assistance, financial support, and procurement services for local governments to implement high-impact clean energy actions.

section_header: ar1. commit to creating, implementing, and updating a comprehensive and equitable state climate change adaptation and resilience plan

parent.policy_statement: Commit to Creating, Implementing, and Updating a Comprehensive and Equitable State Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience Plan (AR1).
parent.verbatim_text: ## AR1.  Commit to Creating, Implementing, and Updating a Comprehensive and Equitable State Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience Plan
parent.section_header: AR1.  Commit to Creating, Implementing, and Updating a Comprehensive and Equitable State Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience Plan
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple implementation components listed below.

subs.policy_statement: Appoint a Chief State Resilience Officer (CSRO) to convene an adaptation and resilience sub-cabinet, oversee all State adaptation/resilience activities including bond expenditures, and ensure just transition principles are incorporated.
subs.verbatim_text: - Provide executive-level coordination of adaptation and resilience activities: The Governor should appoint a Chief State Resilience Officer (CSRO) who would convene an adaptation and resilience sub-cabinet. Importantly, the CSRO's portfolio must include all State adaptation and resilience activities, including oversight of relevant bond fund expenditures, and not be limited to disaster response. The CSRO must ensure incorporation of just transition principles into all adaptation and resilience activities and should report directly to the Director of State Operations.
subs.parent_policy_name: Commit to Creating, Implementing, and Updating a Comprehensive and Equitable State Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience Plan (AR1).
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific executive action (appoint CSRO) and defined governance/oversight requirements.

subs.policy_statement: Develop a comprehensive State climate change adaptation and resilience plan overseen by the CSRO and sub-cabinet, ensuring equitable application and consultation with Indigenous Nations.
subs.verbatim_text: - Develop an adaptation and resilience plan: The CSRO and adaptation and resilience subcabinet should oversee development of a comprehensive State climate change adaptation and resilience plan. This plan and the CSRO should ensure not only that programs are applied equitably, but that, as feasible, adaptation and resilience activities serve to ameliorate environmental, health, social, and economic inequities in historically marginalized communities and in Disadvantaged Communities. Planning should also include consultation with Indigenous Nations.
subs.parent_policy_name: Commit to Creating, Implementing, and Updating a Comprehensive and Equitable State Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience Plan (AR1).
subs.extraction_rationale: Direct commitment to produce a statewide plan with equity and consultation requirements.

subs.policy_statement: Complete preliminary agency vulnerability assessments and adaptation plans and prioritize State adaptation/resilience projects (DEC with OGS support).
subs.verbatim_text: - Complete vulnerability assessments and adaptation plans: New York State Department of Environmental Conservation (DEC), with support from New York State Office of General Services (OGS), should complete preliminary agency vulnerability assessments and adaptation plans and identify and prioritize State adaptation and resilience projects.
subs.parent_policy_name: Commit to Creating, Implementing, and Updating a Comprehensive and Equitable State Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience Plan (AR1).
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete planning deliverables (assessments/plans) and prioritization directive.

subs.policy_statement: Continue the ongoing update to the climate change assessment and initiate/fund additional research (NYSERDA/DEC or other agencies).
subs.verbatim_text: - Continue assessments and research: The New York State Energy Research and Development Authority (NYSERDA) should continue its ongoing update to the climate change assessment, and DEC or other agencies should initiate or fund additional research.
subs.parent_policy_name: Commit to Creating, Implementing, and Updating a Comprehensive and Equitable State Climate Change Adaptation and Resilience Plan (AR1).
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit commitment to ongoing assessment updates and additional research funding.

section_header: ar2. incorporate equitable adaptation and risk-reduction considerations into relevant state funding and regulatory programs, projects, and policies

parent.policy_statement: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
parent.verbatim_text: ## AR2.  Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies
parent.section_header: AR2.  Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies
parent.extraction_rationale: Named strategy with multiple actionable components listed.

subs.policy_statement: Release DEC guidance on projected climatic changes and inequitable risk distribution to support decision-making.
subs.verbatim_text: - Provide guidance on use of climate change projections: DEC should release guidance describing projected climatic changes, including the inequitable distribution of risks, impacts and vulnerabilities, to support relevant decision-making.
subs.parent_policy_name: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
subs.extraction_rationale: Specific deliverable (guidance) with equity content.

subs.policy_statement: Adopt a process to integrate federal/State/local infrastructure investments for efficient and equitable land/resource use and consideration of adaptation/resilience (CSRO recommendations to Executive Chamber).
subs.verbatim_text: - Coordinate infrastructure investments: The CSRO should provide recommendations to the Executive Chamber to adopt a process to ensure integration of federal, State, and local infrastructure investments to ensure efficient and equitable use of land and other resources, addressing historic and present climate and environmental injustices in infrastructure design, siting, and land use decisions, and consideration of adaptation and resilience.
subs.parent_policy_name: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
subs.extraction_rationale: Process change commitment aimed at integrated and equitable infrastructure planning.

subs.policy_statement: Establish goals/processes/criteria and a formal policy to evaluate equity and justice impacts of State adaptation and resilience decisions, and provide guidance to prioritize action in Disadvantaged Communities.
subs.verbatim_text: - Evaluate equity and justice: The State should establish a comprehensive set of goals, processes, and selection criteria for identifying and implementing adaptation and resilience projects that involve meaningful community consultation and empowerment, especially in historically marginalized communities and Disadvantaged Communities; develop a formal policy on evaluation of equity and justice impacts of State adaptation and resilience decisions, including consideration of benefit flows and evaluation of outcomes; and provide guidance on use of such evaluation to prioritize action in Disadvantaged Communities.
subs.parent_policy_name: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
subs.extraction_rationale: Explicit establishment of policy framework and guidance with DAC prioritization.

subs.policy_statement: Convene a work group to adopt climate resilient design guidelines for State-funded projects (OGS and DEC).
subs.verbatim_text: - Adopt resilient design guidelines: OGS and DEC should convene a work group to adopt climate resilient design guidelines for State-funded projects.
subs.parent_policy_name: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
subs.extraction_rationale: Concrete action to create statewide design guidelines via work group.

subs.policy_statement: Amend the Smart Growth Public Infrastructure Policy Act and similar statutes to require consideration of climate hazards and climate equity and develop agency guidance.
subs.verbatim_text: - Amend the Smart Growth Public Infrastructure Policy Act: The State should amend the Smart Growth Public Infrastructure Policy Act and similar statutes to require consideration of climate hazards and climate equity and development of guidance by relevant agencies.
subs.parent_policy_name: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
subs.extraction_rationale: Legislative amendment requirement with specified considerations (hazards, equity) and guidance.

subs.policy_statement: Establish policies/procedures requiring design professionals and contractors on State-funded projects to consider future climate conditions (OGS work group).
subs.verbatim_text: - Enhance design capacity: OGS should convene a work group to establish policies and procedures to require design professionals and contractors on State-funded projects to consider future climate conditions.
subs.parent_policy_name: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
subs.extraction_rationale: Procedural requirement for State-funded projects with concrete implementation step.

subs.policy_statement: Adopt policies ensuring State land and water use plans include climate vulnerability assessments and, as appropriate, resilience/risk-reduction strategies.
subs.verbatim_text: - Assess climate vulnerabilities during land and water planning: DEC,   New York State Department of State (DOS), and other agencies that undertake or fund land or water planning activities should adopt policies to ensure all State land and water use plans include assessment of climate vulnerabilities and, as appropriate, strategies to promote resilience and reduce risk.
subs.parent_policy_name: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
subs.extraction_rationale: Clear policy requirement applied to state plans, including assessment and strategy elements.

subs.policy_statement: Review and potentially strengthen safety codes/standards for manufactured homes and consider additional regulation/incentives/guidance to enhance resilience to climate hazards, coordinated with energy efficiency/renewable access programs where feasible.
subs.verbatim_text: - Enhance resilience of manufactured homes: The State should review current safety codes and standards related to manufactured homes (including mobile homes) and consider the need for additional regulation, incentives or guidance to enhance resilience to climate hazards, particularly flooding, thermal extremes, indoor air quality, and high winds. As feasible, resilience programs for manufactured homes should be coordinated with programs to improve energy efficiency and access to sources of renewable energy.
subs.parent_policy_name: Incorporate Equitable Adaptation and Risk-Reduction Considerations into Relevant State Funding and Regulatory Programs, Projects, and Policies (AR2).
subs.extraction_rationale: Commitment to review codes and consider regulatory/incentive actions for a defined housing type.

section_header: ar3. strengthen meaningful community engagement and public education and build adaptive capacity across all sectors

parent.policy_statement: Strengthen Meaningful Community Engagement and Public Education and Build Adaptive Capacity across all Sectors (AR3).
parent.verbatim_text: ## AR3.  Strengthen Meaningful Community Engagement and Public Education and Build Adaptive Capacity across all Sectors
parent.section_header: AR3.  Strengthen Meaningful Community Engagement and Public Education and Build Adaptive Capacity across all Sectors

subs.parent_policy_name: Strengthen Meaningful Community Engagement and Public Education and Build Adaptive Capacity across all Sectors (AR3).
